_comment: THE LIBRARY (ship-guide goal): the user guide INSIDE the game — every book a focused instruction manual, every book a ROW. ProtoBookPanel renders these; the safehouse BOOKSHELF opens the library; book_* items (items.json) open a single book from the pack. Pages are plain text, ~10 short lines each, bind names written as the DEFAULT keys (F11 rebinds).

books.title: DRIVER'S HANDBOOK
pages:
GETTING MOVING

Walk to any rig and press E to take the wheel.
W is the gas, S the brake/reverse, A/D steer.
SPACE is the HANDBRAKE — at speed it breaks the rear loose into a controlled drift.
Stand on the gas from a standstill and the wheels light up in a BURNOUT.
E steps back out. A passenger can HOLD E to slide over and take the wheel.

THE FIVE PARTS

Every rig has an ENGINE, TIRES, BATTERY, FUEL TANK and CHASSIS — and you FEEL each one fail:
engine misfires, steering slop, flickering lights, a leaking tank.
Salvaged CAR PARTS repair the worst component. A TIRE KIT patches rubber.
A breached tank plus a critical chassis is a fire spiral — get OUT.
Wrecks always burn to a husk. Nothing drives home from that.

FUEL & THE ROAD

The gauge drains as you drive; a JERRY CAN pours +40 fuel into a rig within reach.
Dirt is slower than asphalt — tires decide how much (knobby buggy barely cares, highway van suffers).
Weather matters: rain kills grip, dust kills the headlight cone, heat cooks engines.
In the far wilderness the land ROLLS — heavy states like Colorado are real climbs.
RACING: find a race board, cycle a course with E, and beat your own ghost.

books.title: ON FOOT: THE MOVESET
pages:
MOVING

WASD moves, SHIFT sprints (stamina), CTRL holds a CROUCH — quiet, small, ×0.55 noise.
Sprint + tap CTRL = a SLIDE. SPACE is the combat DIVE.
Walk into water and you wade, then swim. Stamina gone mid-swim is how people drown.
B raises the binoculars. T waits (the clock sprints while you hold still).

UNARMED

LMB tap throws a PUNCH — taps chain into a jab-jab-cross COMBO.
Hold LMB to SHOVE. Sprint + tap = a TACKLE that puts them on the ground.
MARTIAL ARTS levels by doing: lv2 the finisher becomes a KICK, lv4 a shove at
grapple range is a THROW, lv6 punching a downed body is a FINISHER (×3).
Press U to see the whole skill tree light up as you earn it.

ARMED

` cycles weapons; 1/2/3 jump to a slot. R reloads. G throws a grenade.
Right-stick or mouse aims; melee respects walls — no cutting through brick.
Every kill leaves a BODY, not a crate: walk up, E loots the corpse.
Bodies rot away — a picked-clean one goes fast. Cars are weapons too:
anything on the road can be run down. Including you.

books.title: THE PACK: DOGS
pages:
TAKING ONE IN

Strays roam the wastes. E adopts one; P pets it (do this often — the BOND is real).
Feed it dried meat with E. The bond climbs STRAY → COMPANION → PARTNER → SOULBOUND,
and the prompt wears the tier so you always know where you stand.
KINSHIP (your skill) makes every command land faster and taming cheaper.

THE WHISTLE (C)

×1 HEEL — come to me.  ×2 GUARD — hold this spot.
×3 SEEK — sniff out the nearest cache (Hunters can DIG buried ones).
HOLD — SIC: take something down.  ×4 SHIELD — SOULBOUND only: locks to your hip.
Dogs JUMP fences on their own and POUNCE when sicced.

THE DOG'S EYE

Send a PARTNER or SOULBOUND dog seeking and it CARRIES YOUR EYE:
the screen splits as it ranges out — you on one side, the dog's ground on the other.
The tighter the bond, the farther it ranges before the split (SOULBOUND sees farthest).
The eye folds shut when the dog comes off the search.

DOGS ARE MORTAL. Down beside one with a bandage in time, or you dig a grave,
keep the collar, and the kennel wall remembers the name.

books.title: HOME & SURVIVAL
pages:
THE SAFEHOUSE

Meridian's two-story house is yours: supply chest inside, TV in the corner,
drone dock and BUILD BOARD by the door, kennel across the street.
The build board spends scrap: WALLS I–III (raids test them), garage, kennel,
workbench, bed. F drops a beacon pointing home. M is the map; N cycles waypoints.

THE CLOCK & THE BODY

A full day passes in 24 real minutes; the moon decides how dark night gets.
HUNGER drains on the clock — meat, cans, camp-stove meals fix it. Water and
coffee refill stamina; whiskey trades stress for a little torso.
Wounds land on six body parts and TAX you: a bad leg limps, a bad arm shakes your aim.
Bandages patch the worst; a medkit treats everything.

SAVING & GOING DOWN

F5 quick-saves, F9 loads. One file carries the world: dogs, home, ledger, clock.
Going down is NOT the end: you wake at the safehouse, mended — but the
wasteland takes a cut of scrap and scrip, and your rig stays where it fell.
Dogs are the permadeath. Guard them like it.
K opens the character sheet — the whole world, narrated. U is the skill tree.

books.title: GADGETS & SIGNALS
pages:
THE DRONE

USE a scout drone from your pack (TAB) and it patrols overhead, pinging threats.
Use it AGAIN to TAKE THE STICK: your body stands still while you fly with WASD —
and the screen SPLITS as the bird ranges out. You can't switch it off mid-air;
E brings it in to LAND. Attacked while flying? It HOVERS and you snap back.
Docks recharge a drone in a QUARTER OF A DAY — or T-wait the charge away.
The military's HUMVEE carries its own drone bay on the deck.

EYES & EARS

SURVEILLANCE CAMERA: plant it watching a road; V cycles the corner window
to its feed (plant several — V steps camera to camera). E packs it up.
MOTION SENSOR: plant it on an approach; it PINGS you when anything crosses.
WALKIE-TALKIE: key it and LISTEN — movement bleeds through the static
with a compass direction. Dead air means you're alone. Mostly.

RADIO & TV

O opens the RADIO: drag the dial across the FM band — stations lock, static
hisses between them; preset chips jump straight to a frequency. L is next-station,
comma/period the volume. Y SCANS the airwaves for distress calls and warnings.
The safehouse TV: E to sit down, arrows change channels, the slider sets volume.
Five channels broadcast — films, trailers, the EBS when the world turns.

books.title: THE CAROUSEL
pages:
WHAT IT IS

Ten buried military gates ring the country — THE CAROUSEL. A lit gate JUMPS you
across the map. The catch: every gate is a DUNGEON first. Get close and its
OCCUPIERS wake up. The terminal names the price: PURGE the squatters,
buy or earn LAUNCH CODES, socket POWER (jerry cans, power cells).

THE SPIN-UP

Pay the price and the ring starts to SPIN — loud. Waves come to shut it down.
Hold the room until the ring burns teal: the gate is ACTIVE, a reward chest
materializes, and a PORTAL grows on the platform.
Activate the portal and the computer counts you down — ten, nine, eight —
then THE DIAL rolls and you're somewhere else. E at the terminal jumps quietly too.

KEEPING IT

Lit nodes get BESIEGED — relieve them before the deadline or they go dark.
NODE GARAGES: a rig parked at a gate travels with the network.
With Cheyenne's TARGETING CORE the dial stops being roulette:
the jump follows YOUR map course. That's the endgame of the ring.

books.title: THE DIVIDED STATES
pages:
THE COUNTRY

The USA, compressed 60:1 — four hours of real driving crosses it in four minutes.
Every state has a RULER and the border reads your ledger: heroes get welcomes,
problems get BOUNTY HUNTERS. Welcome signs tell you whose law you're under.
RESPECT is per-faction and per-state: prices, standing, and refusals all move with it.

THE CIRCUIT

The loop that keeps you alive: SCAVENGE a cache → UPGRADE at the workbench →
PUSH farther out → light a NODE. The HUD pips track the cycle; finishing one pays.
Y catches radio: distress caches, trade runs, howler warnings.
Weekly, somewhere, a STATE GOES TO WAR — the map is not decoration.

THE NIGHT

Night shrinks what you can see — that's the danger, not a number.
HOWLERS hunt in packs with roles: circlers pin you, chargers come straight,
a SCREAMER calls more. Headlights scare them; so do flares.
LURKERS wait indoors. The walkie hears them moving before you see them.
Respect the dark or feed it.
